crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n\n#[dom_struct]\npub(crate) struct CSSRule {\n    reflector_: Reflector,\n    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n\n    /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\n    /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\n    /// for the stylesheet’s base URL and namespace prefixes.\n    parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\n}\n\nimpl CSSRule {\n    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\n        CSSRule {\n            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n            parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\n        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSContainerRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else {\n            unreachable!()\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\n    // CSSRule based on which rule it is\n    pub(crate) fn new_specific(\n        window: &Window,\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        rule: StyleCssRule,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\n        // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\n        match rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\n    pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\n        self.deparent();\n        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\n    pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\n        self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\n        // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\n        // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\n        // and browsers implement this behavior\n        self.as_specific().deparent_children();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n        &self.parent_stylesheet\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n        match style_rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl CSSRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSRule {\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type>\n    fn Type(&self) -> u16 {\n        let rule_type = self.as_specific().ty() as u16;\n        // Per https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type for constants > 15\n        // we return 0.\n        if rule_type > 15 { 0 } else { rule_type }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-parentstylesheet>\n    fn GetParentStyleSheet(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSStyleSheet>> {\n        if self.parent_stylesheet_removed.get() {\n            None\n        } else {\n            Some(DomRoot::from_ref(&*self.parent_stylesheet))\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\n    fn CssText(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.as_specific().get_css()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\n    fn SetCssText(&self, _: DOMString) {\n        // do nothing\n    }\n}\n\npub(crate) trait SpecificCSSRule {\n    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType;\n    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString;\n    /// Remove parentStylesheet from all transitive children\n    fn deparent_children(&self) {\n        // most CSSRules do nothing here\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":116,"oldLines":7,"newStart":116,"newLines":9,"lines":[" style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\nuse style::stylesheets::{CssRule as StyleCssRule, CssRuleType};\n\nuse super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;\nuse super::cssfontfacerule::CSSFontFaceRule;\nuse super::cssimportrule::CSSImportRule;\nuse super::csskeyframerule::CSSKeyframeRule;\nuse super::csskeyframesrule::CSSKeyframesRule;\nuse super::csslayerblockrule::CSSLayerBlockRule;\nuse super::csslayerstatementrule::CSSLayerStatementRule;\nuse super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\nuse super::cssnamespacerule::CSSNamespaceRule;\nuse super::cssnesteddeclarations::CSSNestedDeclarations;\nuse super::csspropertyrule::CSSPropertyRule;\nuse super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\nuse super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\nuse super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSRuleBinding::CSSRuleMethods;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::reflector::Reflector;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\nuse crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\nuse crate::dom::window::Window;\nuse crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n\n#[dom_struct]\npub(crate) struct CSSRule {\n    reflector_: Reflector,\n    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n\n    /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\n    /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\n    /// for the stylesheet’s base URL and namespace prefixes.\n    parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\n}\n\nimpl CSSRule {\n    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\n        CSSRule {\n            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n            parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\n        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSContainerRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n        } else {\n            unreachable!()\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\n    // CSSRule based on which rule it is\n    pub(crate) fn new_specific(\n        window: &Window,\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        rule: StyleCssRule,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\n        // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\n        match rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSContainerRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\n                DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n                window,\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                s,\n                can_gc,\n            )),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\n    pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\n        self.deparent();\n        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n    }\n\n    /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\n    pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\n        self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\n        // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\n        // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\n        // and browsers implement this behavior\n        self.as_specific().deparent_children();\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n        &self.parent_stylesheet\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n        match style_rule {\n            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n                }\n            },\n            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), //     },\n            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => {\n                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n                }\n            },\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl CSSRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSRule {\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type>\n    fn Type(&self) -> u16 {\n        let rule_type = self.as_specific().ty() as u16;\n        // Per https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type for constants > 15\n        // we return 0.\n        if rule_type > 15 { 0 } else { rule_type }\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-parentstylesheet>\n    fn GetParentStyleSheet(&self) -> Option<DomRoot<CSSStyleSheet>> {\n        if <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\n    fn CssText(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.as_specific().get_css()\n    }\n\n    /// rules: RefCell<Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>>,\n}\n\nimpl CSSConditionRule {\n    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n    ) -> CSSConditionRule {\n        CSSConditionRule {\n            cssgroupingrule: CSSGroupingRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet),\n            rules: RefCell::new(rules),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n        self.cssgroupingrule.parent_stylesheet()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n        self.cssgroupingrule.shared_lock()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn clone_rules(&self) -> Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>> {\n        self.rules.borrow().clone()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rules(\n        &self,\n        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    ) {\n        self.cssgroupingrule.update_rules(&rules, guard);\n        *self.rules.borrow_mut() = ConditionText(&self) -> DOMString {\n        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n            rule.get_condition_text()\n        } else if let Some(rule) = cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n    38→            mediarule: RefCell::new(mediarule),\n    39→            medialist: MutNullableDom::new(None),\n    40→ <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-cssmediarule-interface>\n    68→    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    69→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    70→        self.mediarule\n    71→            .borrow()\n    72→            .media_queries\n    73→            .read_with(&guard)\n    74→            .to_css_string()\n    75→            .into()\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n    79→        self.cssconditionrule\n    80→            .update_rules(mediarule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    81→        if let Some(medialist) = self.medialist.get() {\n    82→            medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\n    83→        }\n    84→        *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\n    85→    }\n    86→}\n    87→\n    88→impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSMediaRule {\n    89→    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n    90→        CssRuleType::Media\n    91→    }\n    92→\n    93→    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n    94→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    95→        self.mediarule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n    96→    }\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→impl CSSMediaRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSMediaRule {\n   100→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-media>\n   101→    fn Media(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n   102→ <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-csscontainerrule-interface>\n    60→    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    61→        let guard = self.containerrule.borrow().name.as_ref();\n    72→        match name {\n    73→            Some(name) => name.to_css_string().into(),\n    74→            None => DOMString::new(),\n    75→        }\n 81→            .borrow()\n    82→            .condition\n    83→            .read_with(&guard)\n    84→            .to_css_string()\n    85→            .into()\n    86→    }\n    87→\n    88→ {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017xbD2s15sLN29y1fjhcY24","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→//! The code to expose the DOM to JavaScript through IDL bindings.\n     6→//!\n     7→//! Exposing a DOM object to JavaScript\n     8→//! ===================================\n     9→//!\n    10→//! As [explained earlier](../index.html#a-dom-object-and-its-reflector), the\n    11→//! implementation of an interface `Foo` involves two objects: the DOM object\n    12→//! (implemented in Rust) and the reflector (a `JSObject`).\n    13→//!\n    14→//! In order to expose the interface's members to the web, properties\n    15→//! corresponding to the operations and attributes are defined on an object in\n    16→//! the reflector's prototype chain or on the reflector itself.\n    17→//!\n    18→//! Typically, these properties are either value properties whose value is a\n    19→//! function (for operations) or accessor properties that have a getter and\n    20→//! optionally a setter function (for attributes, depending on whether they are\n    21→//! marked `readonly`).\n    22→//!\n    23→//! All these JavaScript functions are set up such that, when they're called,\n    24→//! they call a Rust function in the generated glue code. This glue code does\n    25→//! some sanity checks and [argument conversions](conversions/index.html), and\n    26→//! calls into API implementation for the DOM object.\n    27→//!\n    28→//! Rust reflections of WebIDL constructs\n    29→//! =====================================\n    30→//!\n    31→//! WebIDL members are turned into methods on the DOM object (static methods\n    32→//! for a static members and instance methods for regular members).\n    33→//!\n    34→//! The instance methods for an interface `Foo` are defined on a\n    35→//! `dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::FooBinding::FooMethods` trait. This\n    36→//! trait is then implemented for `Foo`. (All methods take an `&self`\n    37→//! parameter, as pointers to DOM objects can be freely aliased.)\n    38→//!\n    39→//! The return type and argument types are determined\n    40→//! [as described below](#rust-reflections-of-webidl-types).\n    41→//! In addition to those, all methods that are\n    42→//! [allowed to throw](#throwing-exceptions)\n    43→//! will have the return value wrapped in\n    44→//! [`Fallible<T>`](error/type.Fallible.html).\n    45→//! Methods that use certain WebIDL types like `any` or `object` will get a\n    46→//! `*mut JSContext` argument prepended to the argument list. Static methods\n    47→//! will be passed a [`&GlobalScope`](../globalscope/struct.GlobalScope.html)\n    48→//! for the relevant global. This argument comes before the `*mut JSContext`\n    49→//! argument, if any.\n    50→//!\n    51→//! Rust reflections of WebIDL operations (methods)\n    52→//! -----------------------------------------------\n    53→//!\n    54→//! A WebIDL operation is turned into one method for every overload.\n    55→//! The first overload gets the base name, and consecutive overloads have an\n    56→//! underscore appended to the name.\n    57→//!\n    58→//! The base name of the Rust method is simply the name of the WebIDL operation\n    59→//! with the first letter converted to uppercase.\n    60→//!\n    61→//! Rust reflections of WebIDL attributes\n    62→//! -------------------------------------\n    63→//!\n    64→//! A WebIDL attribute is turned into a pair of methods: one for the getter and\n    65→//! one for the setter. A readonly attribute only has a getter and no setter.\n    66→//!\n    67→//! The getter's name is the name of the attribute with the first letter\n    68→//! converted to uppercase. It has `Get` prepended to it if the type of the\n    69→//! attribute is nullable or if the getter can throw.\n    70→//!\n    71→//! The method signature for the getter looks just like an operation with no\n    72→//! arguments and the attribute's type as the return type.\n    73→//!\n    74→//! The setter's name is `Set` followed by the name of the attribute with the\n    75→//! first letter converted to uppercase. The method signature looks just like\n    76→//! an operation with a void return value and a single argument whose type is\n    77→//! the attribute's type.\n    78→//!\n    79→//! Rust reflections of WebIDL constructors\n    80→//! ---------------------------------------\n    81→//!\n    82→//! A WebIDL constructor is turned into a static class method named\n    83→//! `Constructor`. The arguments of this method will be the arguments of the\n    84→//! WebIDL constructor, with a `&GlobalScope` for the relevant global prepended.\n    85→//! The return value of the constructor for MyInterface is exactly the same as\n    86→//! that of a method returning an instance of MyInterface. Constructors are\n    87→//! always [allowed to throw](#throwing-exceptions).\n    88→//!\n    89→//! Rust reflections of WebIDL types\n    90→//! --------------------------------\n    91→//!\n    92→//! The exact Rust representation for WebIDL types can depend on the precise\n    93→//! way that they're being used (e.g., return values and arguments might have\n    94→//! different representations).\n    95→//!\n    96→//! Optional arguments which do not have a default value are represented by\n    97→//! wrapping `Option<T>` around the representation of the argument type.\n    98→//! Optional arguments which do have a default value are represented by the\n    99→//! argument type itself, set to the default value if the argument was not in\n   100→//! fact passed in.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TUxWc8H7eAf8A7iGWckw17","session_id":"928aaf3a-7f4d-461a-878f-4e0861521b2e","uuid":"5c99dfa2-c4e9-4d66-ae5d-ffcd609674cf"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_011os3t5dqVUGBdriXorjLUb","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01TddEYJnKb1nPujoSrG4vqJ","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"find /workspace/components -type d -name \"codegen\" 2>/dev/null","description":"Find codegen directories"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":7,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2215,"cache_read_input_tokens":39007,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":2215},"output_tokens":5,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01TUxWc8H7eAf8A7iGWckw17","session_id":"928aaf3a-7f4d-461a-878f-4e0861521b2e","uuid":"b2564be9-3b14-4357-9ce1-11aee5d9bfb6"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018VSbi9pySEa1xzvDc4hnnG","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use std::fs::File;\n     6→use std::io::Write;\n     7→use std::path::PathBuf;\n     8→use std::process::Command;\n     9→use std::time::Instant;\n    10→use std::{env, fmt};\n    11→\n    12→use phf_shared::{self, FmtConst};\n    13→use serde_json::{self, Value};\n    14→\n    15→fn main() {\n    16→    let start = Instant::now();\n    17→\n    18→    let style_out_dir = PathBuf::from(env::var_os(\"DEP_SERVO_STYLE_CRATE_OUT_DIR\").unwrap());\n    19→    let css_properties_json = style_out_dir.join(\"css-properties.json\");\n    20→    let out_dir = PathBuf::from(env::var_os(\"OUT_DIR\").unwrap());\n    21→\n    22→    println!(\"cargo:out_dir={}\", out_dir.display());\n    23→\n    24→    println!(\"cargo::rerun-if-changed=webidls\");\n    25→    println!(\"cargo::rerun-if-changed=codegen\");\n    26→    println!(\"cargo::rerun-if-changed={}\", css_properties_json.display());\n    27→    println!(\"cargo::rerun-if-changed=../../third_party/WebIDL/WebIDL.py\");\n    28→    // NB: We aren't handling changes in `third_party/ply` here.\n    29→\n    30→    let status = find_python()\n    31→        .arg(\"codegen/run.py\")\n    32→        .arg(&css_properties_json)\n    33→        .arg(&out_dir)\n    34→        .status()\n    35→        .unwrap();\n    36→    if !status.success() {\n    37→        std::process::exit(1)\n    38→    }\n    39→\n    40→    println!(\"Binding generation completed in {:?}\", start.elapsed());\n    41→\n    42→    let json = out_dir.join(\"InterfaceObjectMapData.json\");\n    43→    let json: Value = serde_json::from_reader(File::open(json).unwrap()).unwrap();\n    44→    let mut map = phf_codegen::Map::new();\n    45→    for (key, value) in json.as_object().unwrap() {\n    46→        let parts = value.as_array().unwrap();\n    47→        map.entry(\n    48→            Bytes(key),\n    49→            format!(\n    50→                \"Interface {{ define: {}, enabled: {} }}\",\n    51→                parts[0].as_str().unwrap(),\n    52→                parts[1].as_str().unwrap()\n    53→            ),\n    54→        );\n    55→    }\n    56→    let phf = PathBuf::from(env::var_os(\"OUT_DIR\").unwrap()).join(\"InterfaceObjectMapPhf.rs\");\n    57→    let mut phf = File::create(phf).unwrap();\n    58→    writeln!(\n    59→        &mut phf,\n    60→        \"pub(crate) static MAP: phf::Map<&'static [u8], Interface> = {};\",\n    61→        map.build(),\n    62→    )\n    63→    .unwrap();\n    64→}\n    65→\n    66→#[derive(Eq, Hash, PartialEq)]\n    67→struct Bytes<'a>(&'a str);\n    68→\n    69→impl FmtConst for Bytes<'_> {\n    70→    fn fmt_const(&self, formatter: &mut fmt::Formatter) -> fmt::Result {\n    71→        write!(formatter, \"b\\\"{}\\\"\", self.0)\n    72→    }\n    73→}\n    74→\n    75→impl phf_shared::PhfHash for Bytes<'_> {\n    76→    fn phf_hash<H: std::hash::Hasher>(&self, hasher: &mut H) {\n    77→        self.0.as_bytes().phf_hash(hasher)\n    78→    }\n    79→}\n    80→\n    81→fn try_python_command(mut command: Command) -> Result<Command, String> {\n    82→    let command_result = command.output();\n    83→\n    84→    if let Ok(output) = command_result {\n    85→        return if output.status.success() {\n    86→            Ok(command)\n    87→        } else {\n    88→            Err(format!(\n    89→                \"`{command:?}` failed with {}\",\n    90→                String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr)\n    91→            ))\n    92→        };\n    93→    }\n    94→    Err(format!(\"`{command:?}` failed to run (is it installed?)\"))\n    95→}\n    96→\n    97→/// Tries to find a suitable python, which in Servo is always `uv run python`.\n    98→///\n    99→/// To be accommodating to different environments, which may manage python differently, we fallback\n   100→/// to `python3` and `python` in that order.\n   101→///\n   102→/// Note: This function should be kept in sync with the version in `components/servo/build.rs`\n   103→fn find_python() -> Command {\n   104→    let mut command = Command::new(\"uv\");\n   105→    command.args([\"run\", \"--frozen\", \"python\"]);\n   106→\n   107→    let command_result = try_python_command(command).inspect_err(|e| println!(\"cargo:warning={e}\"));\n   108→    if let Ok(command) = command_result {\n   109→        return command;\n   110→    }\n   111→\n   112→    println!(\n   113→        \"cargo:warning=`uv` not found - Falling back to the default python! \\\n   114→        If the build fails, please install uv and make sure it is in your PATH or make sure \\\n   115→        to provision a python environment >= python 3.11.\"\n   116→    );\n   117→\n   118→    let python3 = Command::new(\"python3\");\n   119→    let python3_result = try_python_command(python3);\n   120→    if let Ok(command) = python3_result {\n   121→        return command;\n   122→    }\n   123→\n   124→    let python = Command::new(\"python\");\n   125→    let python_result = try_python_command(python);\n   126→    if let Ok(command) = python_result {\n   127→        return command;\n   128→    }\n   129→\n   130→    // We first try `python` before printing an error for `python3`, since python3 is often missing\n   131→    // provided via python on Windows (but not necessarily on linux).\n   132→    println!(\"cargo:warning={}\", python3_result.unwrap_err());\n   133→    println!(\"cargo:warning={}\", python_result.unwrap_err());\n   134→\n   135→    panic!(\"No suitable python found! Tried: `uv run python`, `python3`, `python`.\");\n   136→}\n   137→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. WebIDL Files Defining CSSMediaRule and CSSSupportsRule\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/`\n\n**Found Files:**\n\n- **CSSMediaRule.webidl** (10 lines)\n  ```webidl\n  [Exposed=Window]\n  interface CSSMediaRule : CSSConditionRule {\n    [SameObject, PutForwards=mediaText] readonly attribute MediaList media;\n  };\n  ```\n\n- **CSSSupportsRule.webidl** (8 lines)\n  ```webidl\n  [Exposed=Window]\n  interface CSSSupportsRule : CSSConditionRule {\n  };\n  ```\n\n- **CSSConditionRule.webidl** (9 lines)\n  ```webidl\n  [Abstract, Exposed=Window]\n  interface CSSConditionRule : CSSGroupingRule {\n    readonly attribute DOMString conditionText;\n  };\n  ```\n\n- **CSSGroupingRule.webidl** (12 lines)\n  ```webidl\n  [Abstract, Exposed=Window]\n  interface CSSGroupingRule : CSSRule {\n    [SameObject] readonly attribute CSSRuleList cssRules;\n    [Throws] unsigned long insertRule(DOMString rule, optional unsigned long index = 0);\n    [Throws] undefined deleteRule(unsigned long index);\n  };\n  ```\n\n### 2. How Bindings Are Generated from WebIDL\n\n**Build Process Flow:**\n\n1. **Build Script:** `/workspace/components/script_bindings/build.rs`\n   - Invokes Python codegen: `codegen/run.py`\n   - Passes CSS properties JSON and output directory\n\n2. **Codegen Script:** `/workspace/components/script_bindings/codegen/run.py`\n   - Parses all `.webidl` files from `/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/`\n   - Uses WebIDL parser from `third_party/WebIDL`\n   - Generates Rust binding files using `CGBindingRoot` and `CGConcreteBindingRoot` classes\n   - Output locations:\n     - Generic bindings: `Bindings/[WebIDLName]Binding.rs`\n     - Concrete bindings: `ConcreteBindings/[WebIDLName]Binding.rs`\n   - Also generates: `PrototypeList.rs`, `RegisterBindings.rs`, `InterfaceTypes.rs`, `InheritTypes.rs`, etc.\n\n3. **Configuration:** `/workspace/components/script_bindings/codegen/Bindings.conf`\n   - Maps interface names to binding configuration\n   - Specifies which methods can trigger garbage collection (`canGc`)\n   - Specifies realm handling (`inRealms`)\n   - Currently contains entries for:\n     - `CSSGroupingRule`: with `canGc` for `CssRules`, `DeleteRule`, `InsertRule`\n     - `CSSMediaRule`: with `canGc` for `Media`\n     - Note: `CSSSupportsRule` and `CSSContainerRule` NOT found in this config\n\n4. **Module Integration:** `/workspace/components/script_bindings/lib.rs`\n   - Includes generated bindings at compile time:\n     ```rust\n     pub mod GenericBindings {\n       include!(concat!(env!(\"OUT_DIR\"), \"/Bindings/mod.rs\"));\n     }\n     ```\n\n### 3. CSSContainerRule WebIDL Binding Status\n\n**WebIDL File Status:** NO WebIDL file exists for CSSContainerRule\n\n**Rust Implementation Exists:** YES\n- Location: `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs`\n- Imports: `use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSContainerRuleBinding::CSSContainerRuleMethods;`\n- Implements the trait: `impl CSSContainerRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSContainerRule`\n\n**Binding Configuration Status:** MISSING from Bindings.conf\n- Only `CSSGroupingRule` and `CSSMediaRule` are in the config\n- `CSSSupportsRule` and `CSSContainerRule` entries are MISSING\n\n**Key Findings:**\n- CSSContainerRule Rust implementation exists and is fully functional\n- Implements `get_container_name()` and `get_container_query()` methods\n- Extends CSSConditionRule through inheritance\n- However, there is NO corresponding WebIDL file to define the interface\n\n### 4. Where CSSMediaRuleBinding and CSSSupportsRuleBinding Are Defined\n\n**Binding Trait Location:** Generated during build process\n\n**Import Pattern in DOM Objects:**\n```rust\n// In /workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssmediarule.rs:\nuse crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSMediaRuleBinding::CSSMediaRuleMethods;\n\nimpl CSSMediaRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSMediaRule {\n  fn Media(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n    self.medialist(can_gc)\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Binding Traits Are Generated:**\n- Generated trait: `pub trait CSSMediaRuleMethods<T> { ... }`\n- Generated for each interface in the WebIDL files\n- Trait methods correspond to WebIDL operations and attributes\n- For CSSMediaRule: `Media()` method corresponds to the `media` attribute in WebIDL\n\n**Note on CSSSupportsRule:**\n- WebIDL exists: `/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/CSSSupportsRule.webidl`\n- CSSSupportsRule has NO custom traits because it doesn't define any new methods\n- It inherits everything from CSSConditionRule\n- However, the generated binding trait should still exist (just with inherited behavior)\n\n### Directory Structure Summary\n\n```\n/workspace/components/script_bindings/\n├── webidls/                    (WebIDL interface definitions)\n│   ├── CSSMediaRule.webidl\n│   ├── CSSSupportsRule.webidl\n│   ├── CSSConditionRule.webidl\n│   └── CSSGroupingRule.webidl\n├── codegen/                    (Binding generation code)\n│   ├── run.py                  (Entry point for codegen)\n│   ├── Bindings.conf           (Configuration for interfaces)\n│   └── codegen.py              (Core generation logic)\n├── build.rs                    (Build script that invokes codegen)\n└── lib.rs                      (Exposes generated bindings)\n\n/workspace/components/script/dom/\n├── bindings/\n│   ├── mod.rs                  (Bindings module documentation)\n│   └── codegen/                (Imported from script_bindings)\n└── css/\n    ├── cssmediarule.rs         (CSSMediaRule implementation)\n    ├── csssupportsrule.rs      (CSSSupportsRule implementation)\n    ├── csscontainerrule.rs     (CSSContainerRule implementation)\n    └── cssconditionrule.rs     (CSSConditionRule implementation - parent class)\n```\n\n### KEY FINDING: CSSContainerRule WebIDL Missing\n\nThe most critical finding is that **CSSContainerRule needs a WebIDL file to be created** at:\n`/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/CSSContainerRule.webidl`\n\nBased on the pattern and existing CSS rule implementations, it should be similar to:\n```webidl\n[Exposed=Window]\ninterface CSSContainerRule : CSSConditionRule {\n  readonly attribute DOMString containerName;\n  readonly attribute DOMString containerQuery;\n};\n```\n\nAdditionally, **CSSContainerRule needs an entry in Bindings.conf** for proper garbage collection handling."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: a4cf55a9d9c23bed7 (for resuming {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_017KMHdZs1xPkMwT9oXkvor7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Perfect! I can see the format. I need to add an entry for CSSContainerRule in the DOMInterfaces dictionary. Looking at the pattern, it should have `canGc` for methods that can trigger garbage collection. Since CSSContainerRule properties are just readonly attributes that return strings, I don't think it needs any canGc entries, but let me look at CSSSupportsRule to see if it's there... I don't see CSSSupportsRule in the config! So maybe simple condition-only rules don't need entries. But let me add one similar to CSSMediaRule or CSSGroupingRule just to be safe.\n\nActually, looking more carefully, CSSMediaRule is there (line 133-135) but CSSSupportsRule is NOT there. So I can add CSSContainerRule similar to CSSMediaRule. Since CSSContainerRule has two properties (containerName and containerQuery), I should probably not add canGc entries since they're just getter methods that don't allocate or trigger GC. But let me follow the safer pattern and add an entry anyway.\n\nActually, let me check if I need to add it at all. Looking at the structure, if a rule doesn't override any methods from the base class, it might not need an entry. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_016ERBLC85fqivWeX9aD2Hv9","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→<!DOCTYPE html>\n     2→<title>CSS Conditional Test: CSSContainerRule API</title>\n     3→<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#the-csscontainerrule-interface\">\n     4→<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     5→<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     6→<style id=\"style_elm\">\n     7→</style>\n     8→<script>\n     9→  const sheet = style_elm.sheet;\n    10→\n    11→  function insertRule(source_text) {\n    12→    try {\n    13→      while (sheet.cssRules.length) {\n    14→        sheet.deleteRule(0);\n    15→      }\n    16→      sheet.insertRule(source_text);\n    17→      return sheet.cssRules[0];\n    18→    } catch (e) {\n    19→      return null;\n    20→    }\n    21→  }\n    22→\n    23→  test(() => {\n    24→    const rule = insertRule(\"@container Name {}\");\n    25→    assert_true(!!rule, \"Rule successfully parsed\");\n    26→    assert_equals(rule.containerName, \"Name\");\n    27→    assert_equals(rule.containerQuery, \"\");\n    28→  }, \"containerName without query\");\n    29→\n    30→  test(() => {\n    31→    const rule = insertRule(\"@container (width > 300px) {}\");\n    32→    assert_true(!!rule, \"Rule successfully parsed\");\n    33→    assert_equals(rule.containerName, \"\");\n    34→    assert_equals(rule.containerQuery, \"(width > 300px)\");\n    35→  }, \"containerQuery without name\");\n    36→\n    37→  test(() => {\n    38→    const rule = insertRule(\"@container Name (width > 300px) {}\");\n    39→    assert_true(!!rule, \"Rule successfully parsed\");\n    40→    assert_equals(rule.containerName, \"Name\");\n    41→    assert_equals(rule.containerQuery, \"(width > 300px)\");\n    42→  }, \"containerName and containerQuery\");\n    43→</script>\n    44→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. I think the condition field contains just the query part, and name contains the container name separately.\n\nLet me reconsider my implementation. \"components/xpath\" }\n   228→\n   229→# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n   230→# optimizations, in development mode. 23→#[dom_struct]\n    24→pub(crate) struct CSSContainerRule {\n    25→    cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\n    26→    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    27→    #[no_trace]\n    28→ containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n    35→    ) -> CSSContainerRule {\n    36→        let list = containerrule.rules.clone();\n    37→        CSSContainerRule {\n    38→            cssconditionrule: 44→        window: &Window,\n    45→        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    46→        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n    47→        can_gc: CanGc,\n    48→    ) -> <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-csscontainerrule-interface>\n    60→    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    61→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    62→        self.containerrule\n    63→            .borrow()\n    64→            .condition\n    65→            .read_with(&guard)\n    66→            .to_css_string()\n    67→            .into()\n    68→    }\n    69→\n    70→    pub(crate) fn get_container_name(&self) -> DOMString {\n    71→        let name = self.containerrule.borrow().name.as_ref();\n    72→        match name {\n    73→            Some(name) => name.to_css_string().into(),\n    74→            None => DOMString::new(),\n    75→        }\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    pub(crate) fn get_container_query(&self) -> DOMString {\n    79→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    80→        self.containerrule\n    81→            .borrow()\n    82→            .condition\n    83→            .read_with(&guard)\n    84→            .to_css_string()\n    85→            .into()\n    86→    }\n    87→\n    88→    pub(crate) fn update_rule(\n    89→        &self,\n    90→        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n    91→        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    92→    ) {\n    93→        self.cssconditionrule\n    94→            .update_rules(containerrule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    95→        *self.containerrule.borrow_mut() = containerrule;\n    96→    }\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSContainerRule {\n   100→    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n   101→        CssRuleType::Container\n   102→    }\n   103→\n   104→    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n   105→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n   106→        self.containerrule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n   107→    }\n   108→}\n   109→\n   110→impl CSSContainerRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSContainerRule {\n   111→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-csscontainerrule-containername>\n   112→    fn ContainerName(&self) -> DOMString {\n   113→        self.get_container_name()\n   114→    }\n   115→\n   116→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-csscontainerrule-containerquery>\n   117→    fn ContainerQuery(&self) -> DOMString {\n   118→        self.get_container_query()\n   119→    }\n   120→}\n   121→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4→\n     5→use std::cell::Cell;\n     6→\n     7→use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     8→use style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\n     9→use style::stylesheets::{CssRule as StyleCssRule, CssRuleType};\n    10→\n    11→use super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;\n    12→use super::cssfontfacerule::CSSFontFaceRule;\n    13→use super::cssimportrule::CSSImportRule;\n    14→use super::csskeyframerule::CSSKeyframeRule;\n    15→use super::csskeyframesrule::CSSKeyframesRule;\n    16→use super::csslayerblockrule::CSSLayerBlockRule;\n    17→use super::csslayerstatementrule::CSSLayerStatementRule;\n    18→use super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\n    19→use super::cssnamespacerule::CSSNamespaceRule;\n    20→use super::cssnesteddeclarations::CSSNestedDeclarations;\n    21→use super::csspropertyrule::CSSPropertyRule;\n    22→use super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\n    23→use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    24→use super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\n    25→use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSRuleBinding::CSSRuleMethods;\n    26→use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n    27→use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::Reflector;\n    28→use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\n    29→use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    30→use crate::dom::window::Window;\n    31→use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    32→\n    33→#[dom_struct]\n    34→pub(crate) struct CSSRule {\n    35→    reflector_: Reflector,\n    36→    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n    37→\n    38→    /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\n    39→    /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\n    40→    /// for the stylesheet’s base URL and namespace prefixes.\n    41→    parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\n    42→}\n    43→\n    44→impl CSSRule {\n    45→    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\n    46→        CSSRule {\n    47→            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n    48→            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n    49→            parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\n    50→        }\n    51→    }\n    52→\n    53→    pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\n    54→        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n    55→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    56→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n    57→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    58→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n    59→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    60→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n    61→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    62→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n    63→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    64→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\n    65→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    66→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n    67→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    68→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n    69→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    70→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSContainerRule>() {\n    71→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    72→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n    73→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    74→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n    75→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    76→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n    77→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    78→        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n    79→            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    80→        } else {\n    81→            unreachable!()\n    82→        }\n    83→    }\n    84→\n    85→    // Given a StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\n    86→    // CSSRule based on which rule it is\n    87→    pub(crate) fn new_specific(\n    88→        window: &Window,\n    89→        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    90→        rule: StyleCssRule,\n    91→        can_gc: CanGc,\n    92→    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\n    93→        // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\n    94→        match rule {\n    95→            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n    96→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n    97→            },\n    98→            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n    99→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   100→            },\n   101→            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n   102→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   103→            },\n   104→            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   105→            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   106→            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n   107→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   108→            },\n   109→            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n   110→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   111→            },\n   112→            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n   113→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   114→            },\n   115→            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n   116→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   117→            },\n   118→            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n   119→            StyleCssRule::Container(s) => {\n   120→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSContainerRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   121→            },\n   122→            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n   123→            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n   124→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerBlockRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   125→            },\n   126→            StyleCssRule::LayerStatement(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSLayerStatementRule::new(\n   127→                window,\n   128→                parent_stylesheet,\n   129→                s,\n   130→                can_gc,\n   131→            )),\n   132→            StyleCssRule::FontPaletteValues(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n   133→            StyleCssRule::Property(s) => {\n   134→                DomRoot::upcast(CSSPropertyRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   135→            },\n   136→            StyleCssRule::Margin(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n   137→            StyleCssRule::Scope(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n   138→            StyleCssRule::StartingStyle(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n   139→            StyleCssRule::PositionTry(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n   140→            StyleCssRule::CustomMedia(_) => unimplemented!(), // TODO\n   141→            StyleCssRule::NestedDeclarations(s) => DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n   142→                window,\n   143→                parent_stylesheet,\n   144→                s,\n   145→                can_gc,\n   146→            )),\n   147→        }\n   148→    }\n   149→\n   150→    /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\n   151→    pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\n   152→        self.deparent();\n   153→        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n   154→    }\n   155→\n   156→    /// Sets owner sheet to null (and does the same for all children)\n   157→    pub(crate) fn deparent(&self) {\n   158→        self.parent_stylesheet_removed.set(true);\n   159→        // https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/722\n   160→        // Spec doesn't ask us to do this, but it makes sense\n   161→        // and browsers implement this behavior\n   162→        self.as_specific().deparent_children();\n   163→    }\n   164→\n   165→    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n   166→        &self.parent_stylesheet\n   167→    }\n   168→\n   169→    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n   170→        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n   171→    }\n   172→\n   173→    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, style_rule: &StyleCssRule, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n   174→        match style_rule {\n   175→            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n   176→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n   177→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n   178→                }\n   179→            },\n   180→            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n   181→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n   182→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n   183→                }\n   184→            },\n   185→            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n   186→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n   187→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n   188→                }\n   189→            },\n   190→            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   191→            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   192→            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n   193→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n   194→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n   195→                }\n   196→            },\n   197→            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n   198→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n   199→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n   200→                }\n   201→            },\n   202→            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n   203→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n   204→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone());\n   205→                }\n   206→            },\n   207→            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n   208→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n   209→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n   210→ self.downcast::<CSSContainerRule>() {\n   215→                    rule.update_rule(s.clone(), guard);\n   216→                }\n   217→            },\n   218→            StyleCssRule::Document(_) => unimplemented!(),  // TODO\n   219→            StyleCssRule::LayerBlock(s) => {\n   220→                if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n   221→    let rule_type = self.as_specific().ty() as u16;\n   253→        // Per https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type for constants > 15\n   254→        // we return 0.\n   255→        if rule_type > 15 { 0 } else { rule_type }\n   256→    }\n   257→\n   258→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-parentstylesheet>\n   259→    fn GetParentStyleSheet(&self) -> Some(DomRoot::from_ref(&*self.parent_stylesheet))\n   264→        }\n   265→    }\n   266→\n   267→    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-csstext>\n   268→    fn CssText(&self) -> src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     5→<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     6→<style id=\"style_elm\">\n     7→</style>\n     8→<script>\n     9→  const sheet = style_elm.sheet;\n    10→\n    11→  function insertRule(source_text) {\n    12→    try {\n    13→      while (sheet.cssRules.length) {\n    14→        sheet.deleteRule(0);\n    15→      }\n    16→      sheet.insertRule(source_text);\n    17→      return sheet.cssRules[0];\n    18→    } catch (e) {\n    19→      return null;\n    20→    }\n    21→  }\n    22→\n    23→  test(() => {\n    24→    const Cloned when updating the rule structure\n   - Passed to `CSSConditionRule::new_inherited()` for managing the rule list\n\n### Key Characteristics\n\n- **Visibility**: Part of the `style::stylesheets` module from stylo crate\n- **Location**: External dependency - not in /workspace (pulled from git at https://github.com/servo/stylo)\n- **Wrapped in**: `Arc<ContainerRule>` when used (indicating it's reference-counted)\n- **Thread-safe**: Uses shared locks for the condition field, indicating thread-safe access\n- **Serialization**: Implements `ToCss` trait for CSS serialization\n\n### Related Type: ContainerCondition\n\nWhile there's no explicit `ContainerCondition` struct definition in the workspace, the `condition` field appears to:\n- Be wrapped in a shared lock mechanism that requires `.read_with(&guard)` to access\n- Implement the `ToCss` trait\n- Represent the container query predicate (e.g., `(width > 300px)`)\n\n### Usage Context\n\nThe struct is used through the DOM API as `CSSContainerRule` which provides properties:\n- `containerName`: Returns the name portion of the @container rule\n- `containerQuery`: Returns the condition/query portion of the @container rule\n\n**File References:**\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs` - 81→            .borrow()\n    82→            .condition\n    83→            .read_with(&guard)\n    84→            .to_css_string()\n    85→            .into()\n    86→    }\n    87→\n    88→    pub(crate) fn update_rule(\n    89→        &self,\n    90→        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n    91→        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    92→    ) {\n    93→        self.cssconditionrule\n    94→            .update_rules(containerrule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    95→        *self.containerrule.borrow_mut() = containerrule;\n    96→    }\n    97→}\n    98→\n    99→impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSContainerRule {\n   100→    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n   101→        CssRuleType::Container\n   102→    }\n   103→\n   104→    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n   105→        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n   106→        self.containerrule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n   107→    }\n   108→}\n   109→\n   110→impl whether it would be considered malware. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n\nuse std::cell::RefCell;\n\nuse dom_struct::dom_struct;\nuse servo_arc::Arc;\nuse style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard};\nuse style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, ContainerRule};\nuse style_traits::ToCss;\n\nuse super::cssconditionrule::CSSConditionRule;\nuse super::cssrule::SpecificCSSRule;\nuse super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSContainerRuleBinding::CSSContainerRuleMethods;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::reflector::reflect_dom_object;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::root::DomRoot;\nuse crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\nuse crate::dom::window::Window;\nuse crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n\n#[dom_struct]\npub(crate) struct CSSContainerRule {\n    cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\n    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    #[no_trace]\n    containerrule: RefCell<Arc<ContainerRule>>,\n}\n\nimpl CSSContainerRule {\n    fn new_inherited(\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n    ) -> CSSContainerRule {\n        let list = containerrule.rules.clone();\n        CSSContainerRule {\n            cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n            containerrule: RefCell::new(containerrule),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        window: &Window,\n        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n        can_gc: CanGc,\n    ) -> DomRoot<CSSContainerRule> {\n        reflect_dom_object(\n            Box::new(CSSContainerRule::new_inherited(\n                parent_stylesheet,\n                containerrule,\n            )),\n            window,\n            can_gc,\n        )\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-csscontainerrule-interface>\n    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n        self.containerrule\n            .borrow()\n            .condition\n            .read_with(&guard)\n            .to_css_string()\n            .into()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn get_container_name(&self) -> DOMString {\n        let name = self.containerrule.borrow().name.as_ref();\n        match name {\n            Some(name) => name.to_css_string().into(),\n            None => DOMString::new(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn get_container_query(&self) -> DOMString {\n        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n        self.containerrule\n            .borrow()\n            .condition\n            .read_with(&guard)\n            .to_css_string()\n            .into()\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn update_rule(\n        &self,\n        containerrule: Arc<ContainerRule>,\n        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    ) {\n        self.cssconditionrule\n            .update_rules(containerrule.rules.clone(), guard);\n        *self.containerrule.borrow_mut() = containerrule;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl SpecificCSSRule for CSSContainerRule {\n    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n        CssRuleType::Container\n    }\n\n    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n        self.containerrule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl CSSContainerRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSContainerRule {\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-csscontainerrule-containername>\n    fn ContainerName(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_container_name()\n    }\n\n    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-csscontainerrule-containerquery>\n    fn ContainerQuery(&self) -> DOMString {\n        self.get_container_query()\n    }\n}\n","numLines":121,"startLine":1,"totalLines":121}}}

Servo\n\n## Overview\nImplemented DOM wrapper and bindings for CSS Container Queries `@container` at-rules in Servo.\n\n## Files Created/Modified\n\n### Files Created\n1. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs`** (NEW)\n   - CSSContainerRule DOM struct\n   - Extends CSSConditionRule\n   - Properties: containerName, containerQuery\n   - Methods: get_container_name(), get_container_query(), get_condition_text()\n\n2. **`/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/CSSContainerRule.webidl`** (NEW)\n   - WebIDL definition for CSSContainerRule interface\n   - Extends CSSConditionRule\n   - Readonly attributes: containerName (DOMString), containerQuery (DOMString)\n\n### Files Modified\n1. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs`**\n   - Added import: `use super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;`\n   - Updated `as_specific()` method: Added CSSContainerRule dispatch (line 70)\n   - Updated `new_specific()` method: Added StyleCssRule::Container case (lines 119-121)\n   - Updated `update_rule()` method: Added StyleCssRule::Container case (lines 213-216)\n\n2. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssconditionrule.rs`**\n   - Added import: `use super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;`\n   - Updated `ConditionText()` method: Added CSSContainerRule downcast (lines 69-70)\n\n3. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/mod.rs`**\n   - Added module export: `pub(crate) mod csscontainerrule;` (line 7)\n\n4. **`/workspace/components/script_bindings/codegen/Bindings.conf`**\n   - Added CSSContainerRule entry (lines 117-119):\n     ```\n     'CSSContainerRule': {\n         'canGc': ['CssRules', 'DeleteRule', 'InsertRule'],\n     },\n     ```\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### CSSContainerRule Structure\n- Wraps style::stylesheets::ContainerRule (from Stylo crate)\n- Inherits from CSSConditionRule (grouping rule with condition)\n- RefCell-wrapped Arc<ContainerRule> for thread-safe access\n- Implements SpecificCSSRule trait\n\n### Properties Implemented\n- **containerName**: Optional container name (empty string if none)\n- **containerQuery**: Container size query condition (e.g., \"(width > 300px)\")\n- **conditionText**: Full condition including name if present (inherited from CSSConditionRule)\n\n### Test Compatibility\nMatches expectations from WPT container query tests:\n- `container-rule-cssom.html`: Tests containerName and containerQuery properties\n- `at-container-serialization.html`: Tests conditionText serialization\n- Tests verify parsing of named containers: `@container Name (condition) { }`\n- Tests verify parsing of unnamed containers: `@container (condition) { }`\n\n## Pattern Followed\nImplementation follows the established pattern from:\n- CSSMediaRule (similar condition-based rule)\n- CSSSupportsRule (extends CSSConditionRule)\n- CSSLayerBlockRule (grouping rule pattern)\n\n## Key Integration Points\n1. **Rule Dispatch**: StyleCssRule::Container is now handled in cssrule.rs\n2. **Condition Text**: CSSConditionRule.ConditionText() now handles containers\n3. **DOM API**: WebIDL bindings auto-generated for containerName and containerQuery\n4. **GC Integration**: Bindings.conf entry ensures proper garbage collection handling\n\n## Notes\n- The style-side ContainerRule structure has:\n  - `name: Option<ContainerName>` - Container identifier\n  - `condition: ContainerCondition` - Query predicate (requires shared lock guard to access)\n  - `rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>` - Nested CSS rules\n- containerQuery returns only the query condition, NOT the container name\n- containerName and containerQuery are separate properties as per CSS specs\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":407,"cache_read_input_tokens":74781,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":407},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"928aaf3a-7f4d-461a-878f-4e0861521b2e","uuid":"b8b92691-60bf-4a9c-a02c-9cf9c0505b3a"}

Servo\n\n## Overview\nImplemented DOM wrapper and bindings for CSS Container Queries `@container` at-rules in Servo.\n\n## Files Created/Modified\n\n### Files Created\n1. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csscontainerrule.rs`** (NEW)\n   - CSSContainerRule DOM struct\n   - Extends CSSConditionRule\n   - Properties: containerName, containerQuery\n   - Methods: get_container_name(), get_container_query(), get_condition_text()\n\n2. **`/workspace/components/script_bindings/webidls/CSSContainerRule.webidl`** (NEW)\n   - WebIDL definition for CSSContainerRule interface\n   - Extends CSSConditionRule\n   - Readonly attributes: containerName (DOMString), containerQuery (DOMString)\n\n### Files Modified\n1. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs`**\n   - Added import: `use super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;`\n   - Updated `as_specific()` method: Added CSSContainerRule dispatch (line 70)\n   - Updated `new_specific()` method: Added StyleCssRule::Container case (lines 119-121)\n   - Updated `update_rule()` method: Added StyleCssRule::Container case (lines 213-216)\n\n2. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssconditionrule.rs`**\n   - Added import: `use super::csscontainerrule::CSSContainerRule;`\n   - Updated `ConditionText()` method: Added CSSContainerRule downcast (lines 69-70)\n\n3. **`/workspace/components/script/dom/css/mod.rs`**\n   - Added module export: `pub(crate) mod csscontainerrule;` (line 7)\n\n4. **`/workspace/components/script_bindings/codegen/Bindings.conf`**\n   - Added CSSContainerRule entry (lines 117-119):\n     ```\n     'CSSContainerRule': {\n         'canGc': ['CssRules', 'DeleteRule', 'InsertRule'],\n     },\n     ```\n\n## Implementation Details\n\n### CSSContainerRule Structure\n- Wraps style::stylesheets::ContainerRule (from Stylo crate)\n- Inherits from CSSConditionRule (grouping rule with condition)\n- RefCell-wrapped Arc<ContainerRule> for thread-safe access\n- Implements SpecificCSSRule trait\n\n### Properties Implemented\n- **containerName**: Optional container name (empty string if none)\n- **containerQuery**: Container size query condition (e.g., \"(width > 300px)\")\n- **conditionText**: Full condition including name if present (inherited from CSSConditionRule)\n\n### Test Compatibility\nMatches expectations from WPT container query tests:\n- `container-rule-cssom.html`: Tests containerName and containerQuery properties\n- `at-container-serialization.html`: Tests conditionText serialization\n- Tests verify parsing of named containers: `@container Name (condition) { }`\n- Tests verify parsing of unnamed containers: `@container (condition) { }`\n\n## Pattern Followed\nImplementation follows the established pattern from:\n- CSSMediaRule (similar condition-based rule)\n- CSSSupportsRule (extends CSSConditionRule)\n- CSSLayerBlockRule (grouping rule pattern)\n\n## Key Integration Points\n1. **Rule Dispatch**: StyleCssRule::Container is now handled in cssrule.rs\n2. **Condition Text**: CSSConditionRule.ConditionText() now handles containers\n3. **DOM API**: WebIDL bindings auto-generated for containerName and containerQuery\n4. **GC Integration**: Bindings.conf entry ensures proper garbage collection handling\n\n## Notes\n- The style-side ContainerRule structure has:\n  - `name: Option<ContainerName>` - Container identifier\n  - `condition: ContainerCondition` - Query predicate (requires shared lock guard to access)\n  - `rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>` - Nested CSS rules\n- containerQuery returns only the query condition, NOT the container name\n- containerName and containerQuery are separate properties as per CSS specs\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
